# UC-22.22.23 · Dormant Account Activity After Long Inactivity

## Description

Provides transaction-level integrity and monitoring evidence aligned with PSD2 operational and security expectations for ASPSPs and payment processors.

## Value

Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for PSD2 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes payments and sourcetypes core:account:activity. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.

## Implementation

(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review. Indexes required: `index=payments`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=core:account:activity`.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk DB Connect (2686).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=payments` `core:account:activity` — authorization_id, merchant_id, amount_eur, fx_rate, reversal_flag.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="core:account:activity" earliest=-24h
| eval txn_tag="dormant"
| eval eur_equiv=coalesce(amount_eur, amount*fx_rate)
| stats count as txns, dc(authorization_id) as auth_dc, sum(eval(eur_equiv>100000)) as very_high by merchant_id, txn_tag, bin(_time,1h)
| eval dup_suspect=if(txns>auth_dc AND txn_tag="dup", txns-auth_dc, 0)
| eval uc_id="22.22.23"
| where very_high>0 OR dup_suspect>5 OR txn_tag="refund"
| sort - very_high
| head 200
```

Understanding this SPL

**Dormant Account Activity After Long Inactivity (PSD2)** — Provides transaction-level integrity and monitoring evidence aligned with PSD2 operational and security expectations for ASPSPs and payment processors.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=payments` `core:account:activity` — authorization_id, merchant_id, amount_eur, fx_rate, reversal_flag. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk DB Connect (2686). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: payments; **sourcetype**: core:account:activity. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=payments, sourcetype="core:account:activity", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **txn_tag** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **eur_equiv** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by merchant_id, txn_tag, bin(_time,1h)** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **dup_suspect** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **uc_id** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where very_high>0 OR dup_suspect>5 OR txn_tag="refund"` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Limits the number of rows with `head`.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with payment switch or bank API logs, fraud case tool output, and the ServiceNow or GRC record for a known test transaction or drill window.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Column chart (very_high), Table (merchant_id), Single value (dup_suspect)

## SPL

```spl
index=payments sourcetype="core:account:activity" earliest=-24h
| eval txn_tag="dormant"
| eval eur_equiv=coalesce(amount_eur, amount*fx_rate)
| stats count as txns, dc(authorization_id) as auth_dc, sum(eval(eur_equiv>100000)) as very_high by merchant_id, txn_tag, bin(_time,1h)
| eval dup_suspect=if(txns>auth_dc AND txn_tag="dup", txns-auth_dc, 0)
| eval uc_id="22.22.23"
| where very_high>0 OR dup_suspect>5 OR txn_tag="refund"
| sort - very_high
| head 200
```
